nodes.context: The overarching criminal organization comprising Italian-American crime families, governed by The Commission.
Governing body of the American Mafia, formed in 1931, consisting of bosses of major crime families.
The five major Mafia families in New York City: Genovese, Gambino, Lucchese, Bonanno, and Colombo.
The Mafia family based in Chicago, a member of The Commission.
One of the Five Families of New York, holds a Commission seat from 1931 to present.
One of the Five Families of New York, holds a Commission seat from 1931 to present.
One of the Five Families of New York, holds a Commission seat from 1931 to present.
One of the Five Families of New York, held a Commission seat from 1931 to 1980s and again from 1990s to present.
One of the Five Families of New York, holds a Commission seat from 1931 to 1990s and again from 2000s to present.
Mafia family based in Buffalo, held a Commission seat from 1931 to 1974, represented by Genovese family.
Mafia family based in Philadelphia, held a Commission seat from 1961 to 1980, represented by Genovese family.
Mafia family based in Detroit, held a Commission seat from 1961 to 1977, represented by Genovese family.
Mafia family based in New England, represented by Genovese family on the Commission.
Mafia family based in New Jersey, represented by Genovese family on the Commission.
Mafia family based in Pittsburgh, represented by Genovese family on the Commission.
Mafia family based in Cleveland, represented by Genovese family on the Commission.
Mafia family based in New Orleans, represented by Genovese family on the Commission.
Mafia family based in Rochester, defunct, represented by Genovese family on the Commission.
Mafia family based in Dallas, represented by Genovese family on the Commission.
Mafia family based in Pennsylvania, represented by Colombo family on the Commission.
Mafia family based in Milwaukee, represented by Chicago Outfit on the Commission.
Mafia family based in Kansas City, represented by Chicago Outfit on the Commission.
Mafia family based in St. Louis, represented by Chicago Outfit on the Commission.
Mafia family based in Tampa, represented by Chicago Outfit on the Commission.
Mafia family based in Los Angeles, held a Commission seat from 1931 to 1956, represented by Chicago Outfit.
Mafia family based in San Francisco, represented by Chicago Outfit on the Commission.
Mafia family based in San Jose, represented by Chicago Outfit on the Commission.
Jewish-Italian organized crime group that acted as the enforcement arm of the National Crime Syndicate.
Mafia family based in Hamilton, Ontario, Canada.
Governing body of the American Mafia, formed in 1931, overseeing Mafia activities in the US.
The five major Mafia families in New York City.
Mafia family based in Chicago, part of the Commission.
Mafia family from New England, part of the Commission at various times.
Mafia family from Buffalo, part of the Commission at various times.
Mafia family from Philadelphia, part of the Commission at various times.
Mafia family from Detroit, part of the Commission at various times.
Mafia family based in Montreal, Canada.

edges.evidence_quote: The Commission is the governing body of the American Mafia
edges.context: The Commission governs the American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: a ruling committee that consists of the bosses of the Five Families of New York City
edges.context: The Commission includes the bosses of the Five Families.

edges.evidence_quote: as well as the bosses of the Chicago Outfit
edges.context: The Commission includes the boss of the Chicago Outfit.

edges.evidence_quote: Buffalo family boss Stefano Magaddino
edges.context: The Buffalo family boss was a member of the Commission.

edges.evidence_quote: the crime families of Philadelphia, headed by Angelo Bruno, and Detroit, headed by Joseph Zerilli, were added to the Commission
edges.context: Philadelphia crime family was added to the Commission in 1956.

edges.evidence_quote: the crime families of Philadelphia, headed by Angelo Bruno, and Detroit, headed by Joseph Zerilli, were added to the Commission
edges.context: Detroit crime family was added to the Commission in 1956.

edges.context: Genovese family holds a seat on the Commission.

edges.context: Lucchese family holds a seat on the Commission.

edges.evidence_quote: Colombo (1931–1990s; 2000s–present)
edges.context: Colombo family held a seat on the Commission from 1931 to 1990s and again from 2000s.

edges.evidence_quote: the Commission ordered Lepke Buchalter to eliminate Schultz
edges.context: The Commission ordered Murder, Inc. leader Lepke to kill Dutch Schultz.

edges.evidence_quote: Joe Bonanno made plans to assassinate several rivals on the Commission—bosses Tommy Lucchese, Carlo Gambino, and Stefano Magaddino, as well as Frank DeSimone. Bonanno sought Profaci crime family boss Joseph Magliocco's support
edges.context: Bonanno conspired with Profaci (Colombo) boss Magliocco to assassinate Commission rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: Joe Bonanno made plans to assassinate several rivals on the Commission—bosses Tommy Lucchese
edges.context: Bonanno planned to assassinate Lucchese boss Tommy Lucchese.

edges.evidence_quote: Joe Bonanno made plans to assassinate several rivals on the Commission—bosses Tommy Lucchese, Carlo Gambino
edges.context: Bonanno planned to assassinate Gambino boss Carlo Gambino.

edges.evidence_quote: Joe Bonanno made plans to assassinate several rivals on the Commission—bosses ... Stefano Magaddino
edges.context: Bonanno planned to assassinate Buffalo boss Stefano Magaddino.

edges.evidence_quote: Chicago Outfit (1931–present), often represented by the Genovese family
edges.context: The Chicago Outfit is often represented by the Genovese family on the Commission.
